ogTitle: Sevenhill vs Shea-Oak Log: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Sevenhill and Shea-Oak Log.
heading: Sevenhill vs Shea-Oak Log — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sevenhill, South Australia and Shea-Oak Log, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sevenhill has a lower overall climate risk score (28/100) compared to Shea-Oak Log (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sevenhill or Shea-Oak Log safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sevenhill has a flood risk score of 26/100, while Shea-Oak Log scores 25/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sevenhill scores 16/100 for bushfire risk. Shea-Oak Log scores 31/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sevenhill or Shea-Oak Log?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sevenhill has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
